headline: DoubleVerify (DV) Launches AI-Powered Pre-Screen Content Controls on Meta (META) Threads Feed
summary: DoubleVerify Holdings Inc. (NYSE:DV) is one of the cheap AI stocks to buy according to analysts. On May 18, DoubleVerify launched AI-powered pre-screen content controls on Meta’s Threads feed to enhance brand protection for advertisers. This capability allows brands to avoid content they deem unsuitable before impressions are transacted, building upon DV’s existing post-bid brand […]

headline: Meta Legal Pressures Mount As Shares Trade Below Analyst Valuations
summary: Meta Platforms (NasdaqGS:META) has reached the first settlement with a U.S. school district over claims that its social media products harmed student mental health, in a case with more than 1,200 similar lawsuits pending. The Texas Attorney General has filed a new lawsuit accusing Meta of misleading consumers about WhatsApp’s encryption and privacy protections. A European court has ruled that Meta must compensate publishers for news snippets used on its platforms and for training its AI...

headline: “We’re Studying You to Figure Out How to Make This All More Effective” — Zuckerberg’s Leaked Comment Sparks Job Loss Fears
summary: Mark Zuckerberg said what every mega-cap CEO is probably thinking but few will say on tape. In leaked audio from an April 30, 2026 Meta all-hands, the CEO told employees: “The AI models learn from watching really smart people do things. headline: GDP Alert: The United Kingdom Is Poorer Than Every Individual U.S. State

headline: Applied Materials Taps UCLA AI Chip Hub As Valuation Debate Grows
summary: Applied Materials (NasdaqGS:AMAT) is joining Meta, Broadcom, GlobalFoundries, and Synopsys to fund a new US$125 million semiconductor research hub at UCLA. The hub is focused on next generation chip and AI technologies and is intended to support a more resilient U.S. semiconductor supply chain. This multi company partnership positions Applied Materials at the center of collaborative research across equipment, design, foundry, and end user segments. For you as an investor, this move...

headline: Meta Forum Launch Puts Fresh Spotlight On Reddit’s Growth Story
summary: Meta has launched a new discussion platform called Forum, directly targeting Reddit style community conversations. The app is positioned as a standalone product focused on forum based groups and topic threads, similar to Reddit’s core model. This move introduces fresh competitive pressure on Reddit’s communities and user engagement at a time when investors are closely tracking NYSE:RDDT. For investors watching NYSE:RDDT, Meta’s Forum launch arrives as Reddit trades around $141.67, with the...
